source: H-optimus-0 whole-slide inference -> Claude(Sonnet) K-Pro answer (cached from end2end_ho.py)
prompt: Characterize the tumor microenvironment in this slide.
ho_composition: TUM 38%, STR 19%, LYM 19%, NORM 9%, MUS 7%, DEB 7%
answer: This colorectal H&E slide is dominated by tumor epithelium (38%): moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma glands embedded within a substantial desmoplastic stroma (19%) of reactive spindle-cell fibroblasts and collagen. A prominent lymphocytic infiltrate (19%) matches the stromal bulk, a brisk peritumoral immune response. Residual normal colonic mucosa (9%) and smooth muscle (7%) sit at the margin, suggesting invasion into the muscle wall, and scattered necrotic debris (7%) reflects central tumor necrosis. Overall a moderately immune-infiltrated, desmoplastic microenvironment with notable lymphocytic engagement relative to stromal bulk.
